expectations.evidence: old_skill session-brief.md shows '[LOADED:woocommerce:9.x] — from plugins/beacon/technologies/woocommerce/9.x.md'. The tech pack was loaded (version 9.x, not 10.x as detected by with_skill).

expectations.text: At least one of /wp-json/wc/store/v1/products or /wp-json/wc/v3/products probed
expectations.evidence: old_skill session-brief.md endpoint table shows both '/wp-json/wc/v3/products' (Consumer Key, 401) and '/wp-json/wc/store/v1/products' (200, 100 products). Both WooCommerce API surfaces were probed.

expectations.text: api-surfaces/ directory contains at least one file
expectations.evidence: outputs/docs/research/woocommerce-com/api-surfaces/woocommerce-store-api.md exists. File present in directory listing.

claims.evidence: old_skill loaded woocommerce:9.x but with_skill detected WooCommerce 10.7.0-rc.1 from the wcAnalytics JS global on the same target. The 9.x load appears to be a tech pack version mismatch — the old skill may have detected an incorrect or outdated version.

Session brief states '200 - 100 products' but this cannot be independently verified from output files. with_skill reports 5 products from the same endpoint, suggesting the 100 number may be inaccurate or reflect pagination.

uncertainties: Version discrepancy: old_skill reports WooCommerce 9.x while with_skill detects 10.7.0-rc.1 from the same site

suggestions.reason: Both with_skill and old_skill pass all 3 assertions despite loading different WooCommerce versions (10.x vs 9.x). The eval does not check version accuracy. Adding an assertion like 'tech pack version matches detected wcAnalytics.woo_version' would make this eval discriminating enough to catch the version detection gap.

eval_feedback.overall: This eval passes for old_skill, meaning it does not differentiate the two skill versions on e-commerce probe quality. The version detection gap (9.x vs 10.x) is a meaningful behavioral difference that goes completely unchecked.
